item.title: Powwow Dance Classes
item.description:
Thursday, May 28, 2026 at 6:30 PM

Location: Daybreak Star Indian Cultural Center\, 5011 Bernie Whitebear Way\, Seattle\, WA\, 98199\, United States

We are proud to announce that Powwow Dance Classes have returned! This 19 week program is open to all Indigenous families and individuals. Participants will dive deep into the protocols: history: and cultural significance of the Powwow. This series offers a unique opportunity for dancers to be honored during a special (optional) Coming Out Ceremony at the 37th Annual Indian Days Powwow. Workshop Details 📅 Dates: Thursdays from February 19th through July 2nd (UPDATE: There will be class on March 12\, 2026) ⏰ Time: 6:30 PM to 8:30 PM 📍 Location: Daybreak Star Cultural Center (5011 Bernie Whitebear Way: Seattle: WA) 🍎 Refreshments provided! Dance Styles Taught Mens’ Styles: Grass: Fancy: and Traditional Womens’ Styles: Jingle: Fancy: and Traditional Meet the Instructors Lauren Nabahe (Paiute Shoshone: Navajo: Mono) Jobey T. Williams (Tulalip and Yakama) Contact and More Information For questions or further details: please reach out via email at: Events@unitedindians.org View the event flyer here: https://www.facebook.com/share/p/1KSaA6TiNb/

From United Indians / Daybreak Star

item.title: Veteran Benefits 101 Workshop Series
item.description:
Friday, May 29, 2026 at 11 AM

Location: Labateyah Youth Home\, 9010 13th Ave NW\, Seattle\, WA\, 98117\, United States

Our Native Veterans & Elders Programs are teaming up with the WA’s VA to bring you a Veteran Benefits 101 Workshop series! Whether you are looking to learn about your eligibility or ready to start an application onsite with an accredited Veteran Service Officer\, we are here to support you! Share this workshop series with the veterans in your community! Event Details When: The last Friday of each month Time: 11 am to 1 pm Where: Labateyah Youth Home\, 9010 13th Ave NW\, Seattle\, WA 98117 Upcoming Workshop Topics March 27: Veterans Benefits Administration 101 (Disability Compensation\, Education\, Home Loans\, Burials\, & more) April 24: Veterans Health Administration 101 (VA Hospital & Medical Care) May 29: Veteran Service Officer Networking & Additional Benefits 101 What to Expect Lunch provided for attendees Gift cards available for veterans\, veteran spouses\, or caretakers of veterans (Limit 1 per household) Transportation is available\, if needed Registration is encouraged to help us prepare: https://forms.office.com/r/KrRTQ86ZPr   Questions? Please email us at veterans@unitedindians.org Note: Workshop topics and schedules are subject to change. #SeattleVeterans #SeattleEvents #WashingtonVA #SeattleSeniors #VeteranBenefits #VABenefits #ServiceOfficers #VeteranSupport #MilitaryFamily #Veterans101 #VeteranResources

From United Indians / Daybreak Star

item.title: Drawing From History: Ripples of Time
item.description:
Saturday, May 30, 2026 at 1 PM

Location: Microsoft Lakefront Pavilion\, MOHAI - 860 Terry Ave N\, Seattle\, WA\, 98109\, United States

Lake Union is haunted by ghosts of the past\, echoing through time. In this class\, participants will explore past\, present\, and future of the Lake Union Watershed by creating quick layered gesture drawings in charcoal\, based off photos and ephemera from MOHAI’s collections. The class will culminate at the water’s edge\, creating a drawing representing visions for the future. \nDrawing From History is offered in partnership with Gage Academy of Art. \nAll art making materials will be provided. Tickets include access to MOHAI’s exhibitions. For accessibility support and general program inquiries\, email programs@mohai.org.

From MOHAI - Museum of History & Industry

item.title: Powwow Dance Classes
item.description:
Thursday, June 4, 2026 at 6:30 PM

Location: Daybreak Star Indian Cultural Center\, 5011 Bernie Whitebear Way\, Seattle\, WA\, 98199\, United States

We are proud to announce that Powwow Dance Classes have returned! This 19 week program is open to all Indigenous families and individuals. Participants will dive deep into the protocols: history: and cultural significance of the Powwow. This series offers a unique opportunity for dancers to be honored during a special (optional) Coming Out Ceremony at the 37th Annual Indian Days Powwow. Workshop Details 📅 Dates: Thursdays from February 19th through July 2nd (UPDATE: There will be class on March 12\, 2026) ⏰ Time: 6:30 PM to 8:30 PM 📍 Location: Daybreak Star Cultural Center (5011 Bernie Whitebear Way: Seattle: WA) 🍎 Refreshments provided! Dance Styles Taught Mens’ Styles: Grass: Fancy: and Traditional Womens’ Styles: Jingle: Fancy: and Traditional Meet the Instructors Lauren Nabahe (Paiute Shoshone: Navajo: Mono) Jobey T. Williams (Tulalip and Yakama) Contact and More Information For questions or further details: please reach out via email at: Events@unitedindians.org View the event flyer here: https://www.facebook.com/share/p/1KSaA6TiNb/

From United Indians / Daybreak Star

item.title: Pet First Aid
item.description:
Saturday, June 6, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: Metro Dog\, 4020 23rd Ave W\, Seattle\, WA\, 98119\, United States

Join us for a day of the most comprehensive pet first aid course available! Our Walks ‘n’ Wags certified instructors offer a day of fun and learning. Classes are intended for pet care professionals\, dog and cat owners\, rescue workers\, first responders\, groomers and shelter workers. Course completion offers continuing education credits for PSI\, IAABC\, IACP and CBCC-KA members. This class has received the Animal Wellness Magazine Stamp of Approval and a Modern Dog Fave Find. We work with live animals in class.

From Discover Magnolia

item.title: Meet Up Magnolia- virtual networking
item.description:
Thursday, June 11, 2026 at 9:30 AM

Location: https://us02web.zoom.us/j/85388228818?pwd=amdmUEMvcVo2YjhETkxkbGJCa1Q4Zz09

Our member businesses know the value of networking. Join us for our virtual networking event\, Meet Up Magnolia\, on the  2nd Thursday of every month. \nHow does this virtual networking work? You simply log on to the Zoom link and will be paired into one-on-one chats for 8 to 10 minutes each. We will repeat the process for approximately 3 or 4 rounds. You even have the option to follow up after the meeting and send a message to one or more of the people you meet to keep the dialogue going. \nSo many people to meet\, so many professional relationships to build. Join us HERE.

From Discover Magnolia

item.title: Powwow Dance Classes
item.description:
Thursday, June 11, 2026 at 6:30 PM

Location: Daybreak Star Indian Cultural Center\, 5011 Bernie Whitebear Way\, Seattle\, WA\, 98199\, United States

We are proud to announce that Powwow Dance Classes have returned! This 19 week program is open to all Indigenous families and individuals. Participants will dive deep into the protocols: history: and cultural significance of the Powwow. This series offers a unique opportunity for dancers to be honored during a special (optional) Coming Out Ceremony at the 37th Annual Indian Days Powwow. Workshop Details 📅 Dates: Thursdays from February 19th through July 2nd (UPDATE: There will be class on March 12\, 2026) ⏰ Time: 6:30 PM to 8:30 PM 📍 Location: Daybreak Star Cultural Center (5011 Bernie Whitebear Way: Seattle: WA) 🍎 Refreshments provided! Dance Styles Taught Mens’ Styles: Grass: Fancy: and Traditional Womens’ Styles: Jingle: Fancy: and Traditional Meet the Instructors Lauren Nabahe (Paiute Shoshone: Navajo: Mono) Jobey T. Williams (Tulalip and Yakama) Contact and More Information For questions or further details: please reach out via email at: Events@unitedindians.org View the event flyer here: https://www.facebook.com/share/p/1KSaA6TiNb/

From United Indians / Daybreak Star

item.title: Unpaving SODO Walking Tour
item.description:
Friday, June 12, 2026 at 6 PM

Location: Offsite

What does SODO mean to you? Industrial space\, maybe sports\, perhaps a no-man’s land between Pioneer Square’s nightlife and the cities further south. How do we even define this territory south of downtown where the Duwamish watershed meets the Puget Sound? A liminal space that began with the natural foundation of tidal flats and the flow of a river. An urban landscape that has been literally built on the dreams of manifest destiny. It has been occupied and enjoyed by the commercial minded and marginalized alike – those pushing boundaries and those enforcing them. \nJoin Invisible Histories on a 1-mile immersive guided walking tour of the cityscape you pass daily but never truly see. Storytelling\, history and theatrical elements will enliven these spaces known as SODO. Guests will uniquely experience the past\, from the untold stories of Seattle’s evolving landscapes and industries\, to the hidden histories of the depression era Hooverville\, all the way to the energetic underground club scene of the 1990s. Explore the layers beneath the pavement while we bring history to life like never before! \nThis tour requires walking between multiple sites in SODO. For any accessibility support\, or general inquiries\, email programs@mohai.org.

From MOHAI - Museum of History & Industry

item.title: History Café: Shelf Life Community Story Project
item.description:
Wednesday, June 17, 2026 at 6:30 PM

Location: Faye G. Allen Grand Atrium\, MOHAI 860 Terry Ave N\, Seattle\, WA\, 98109\, United States

The Shelf Life Community Story Project records oral histories with current and former residents of Seattle’s Central District neighborhood. Join contributors for a talk about their new book\, Limitless: Stories from the Neighborhood that Shaped Seattle\, the culmination of a multi-year oral history project in Seattle’s historically redlined Central District neighborhood. Stories of innovation\, interdependence\, self-determination\, creativity\, and resistance are illustrated by seven artists with roots in the CD. \nHistory Café is produced as a partnership between HistoryLink\, The Pacific Northwest Historians Guild\, and MOHAI. \nASL Interpretation and CART captioning are available during the program. In addition\, a limited number of Assistive Listening Devices are available upon request. For more accessibility support\, email programs@mohai.org before the program. \nGrab some food for thought–complimentary snacks and beverages will be provided. \nIf you can’t make it to the museum\, tune in live on the MOHAI YouTube channel to virtually attend this event.

From MOHAI - Museum of History & Industry

Join the community for a series of monthly 5K runs and walks held at the historic Daybreak Star Indian Cultural Center. This free event welcomes participants of all levels\, from competitive runners to those who enjoy a leisurely walk through beautiful scenery. Event Schedule Events take place on the 3rd Sunday of each month. Please note the following upcoming dates: February 15th March 15th April 19th May 17th June 21st July 19th (37th Annual Indian Days Powwow Weekend\, starting at 9:00 AM) Timing and Location Details Start Time: 10:00 AM Location: Daybreak Star Indian Cultural Center (5011 Bernie Whitebear Way Seattle\, WA 98199) Meeting Point: Meet at the Powwow Grounds This event is FREE for all! Lace up your shoes and enjoy the scenic trails while connecting with the community! For more information about the venue and organizing group\, please email communications@unitedindians.org

From United Indians / Daybreak Star

item.title: Powwow Dance Classes
item.description:
Thursday, June 25, 2026 at 6:30 PM

Location: Daybreak Star Indian Cultural Center\, 5011 Bernie Whitebear Way\, Seattle\, WA\, 98199\, United States

We are proud to announce that Powwow Dance Classes have returned! This 19 week program is open to all Indigenous families and individuals. Participants will dive deep into the protocols: history: and cultural significance of the Powwow. This series offers a unique opportunity for dancers to be honored during a special (optional) Coming Out Ceremony at the 37th Annual Indian Days Powwow. Workshop Details 📅 Dates: Thursdays from February 19th through July 2nd (UPDATE: There will be class on March 12\, 2026) ⏰ Time: 6:30 PM to 8:30 PM 📍 Location: Daybreak Star Cultural Center (5011 Bernie Whitebear Way: Seattle: WA) 🍎 Refreshments provided! Dance Styles Taught Mens’ Styles: Grass: Fancy: and Traditional Womens’ Styles: Jingle: Fancy: and Traditional Meet the Instructors Lauren Nabahe (Paiute Shoshone: Navajo: Mono) Jobey T. Williams (Tulalip and Yakama) Contact and More Information For questions or further details: please reach out via email at: Events@unitedindians.org View the event flyer here: https://www.facebook.com/share/p/1KSaA6TiNb/

From United Indians / Daybreak Star
